vocab.meaning: bắt đầu một sở thích hay thói quen mới
vocab.example: I actually took up painting as a toddler because my parents wanted to encourage my creative side.

vocab.meaning: nghịch ngợm, thử nghiệm một cách không nghiêm túc
vocab.example: In primary school, I used to mess around with watercolours just to see how the different pigments merged on paper.

vocab.meaning: phác thảo nhanh những nét chính
vocab.example: I remember spending hours in my bedroom trying to sketch out the characters from my favorite comic books.

vocab.meaning: học kỹ năng mới một cách tự nhiên thông qua luyện tập
vocab.example: I managed to pick up some basic shading techniques just by watching my older sister work on her art projects.

vocab.meaning: tô màu vào các khoảng trống
vocab.example: As a young child, I found it incredibly therapeutic to fill in complex patterns in my coloring books.

vocab.meaning: làm sạch/rửa trôi (nghĩa bóng: giải tỏa căng thẳng)
vocab.example: Painting was a great way to wash away the stresses of a demanding school day, even when I was quite young.
I often look back on those early art classes with a lot of fondness, even though I wasn't particularly talented.

vocab.meaning: từ bỏ một thói quen hay đam mê
vocab.example: Unfortunately, I had to give up drawing in high school to focus more on my academic subjects.

vocab.meaning: lùi lại để quan sát kỹ hơn (tổng thể)
vocab.example: My teacher taught me to stand back from my easel every ten minutes to evaluate the proportions of my work.

vocab.meaning: truyền lại (kinh nghiệm/đam mê) cho thế hệ sau
vocab.example: My father is a professional artist, so he passed down his love for oil painting to me at a very tender age.
